After.title: After
After.description: Check if event timestamp is after specific time.

AggregatedTimestamps.title: AggregatedTimestamps
AggregatedTimestamps.description:
Response model containing info about aggregated timestamps
generated by input plugins.

Attributes
----------
span_edges : list[datetime]
    Timestamps representing edges of spans in aggregation histogram.

span_counts : dict[int, list[int]]
    Count of timestamps within spans for each plugin id.

total : int
    Total count of timestamps

first_timestamps : list[datetime] | None
    First x timestamps from sample.

last_timestamps : list[datetime] | None
    Last x timestamps from sample.

timestamps : list[datetime] | None
    All timestamps from sample.

Notes
-----
All timestamps sample is provided if its size is not that much
(<= 100), otherwise first_timestamps and last_timestamps are
provided with size of 50 each.

And-Input.title: And
And-Input.description:
Logic operator 'and' for combining checks or other logic
operators.

And-Output.title: And
And-Output.description:
Logic operator 'and' for combining checks or other logic
operators.

ApiGeneratorConfig-Input.title: ApiGeneratorConfig
ApiGeneratorConfig-Input.description:
Type-resolved generator config that also accepts placeholders.

Attributes
----------
input : list[ApiInputPluginNamedConfig]
    List of input plugin named configurations.

event : ApiEventPluginNamedConfig
    Event plugin named configuration.

output : list[ApiOutputPluginNamedConfig]
    List of output plugin named configurations.

Notes
-----
Unlike ``runtime_types.GeneratorConfig`` which rejects placeholder
strings, this model accepts ``${params.*}`` and ``${secrets.*}``
in any field.  The OpenAPI schema still documents every plugin
field with its original type.

ApiGeneratorConfig-Output.title: ApiGeneratorConfig
ApiGeneratorConfig-Output.description:
Type-resolved generator config that also accepts placeholders.

Attributes
----------
input : list[ApiInputPluginNamedConfig]
    List of input plugin named configurations.

event : ApiEventPluginNamedConfig
    Event plugin named configuration.

output : list[ApiOutputPluginNamedConfig]
    List of output plugin named configurations.

Notes
-----
Unlike ``runtime_types.GeneratorConfig`` which rejects placeholder
strings, this model accepts ``${params.*}`` and ``${secrets.*}``
in any field.  The OpenAPI schema still documents every plugin
field with its original type.

AuthParameters.title: AuthParameters
AuthParameters.description:
Authentication parameters.

Attributes
----------
user : str, default='eventum'
    User for basic auth.

password : str, default='eventum'
    Password for basic auth.

BatchParameters.title: BatchParameters
BatchParameters.description:
Batcher parameters.

Attributes
----------
size : int | None, default=10000
    Batch size for generating events.

delay : float | None, default=1.0
    Batch delay (in seconds) for generating events.

Notes
-----
At least one parameter must be not `None`.

Before.title: Before
Before.description: Check if event timestamp is before specific time.

BulkStartResponse.title: BulkStartResponse
BulkStartResponse.description:
Response model that contains info about running and non running
generator ids after bulk start operation.

Attributes
----------
running_generator_ids : list[str]
    List of ids of running generators.

non_running_generator_ids : list[str]
    List of ids of non running generators.

CSVSampleConfig.title: CSVSampleConfig
CSVSampleConfig.description:
Configuration of csv sample.

Attributes
----------
type : Literal[SampleType.CSV]
    Discriminator field for sample configuration.

header : bool, default=False
    Whether the provided csv sample includes header.

delimiter : str, default=','
    Delimiter for csv values.

quotechar : str, default='"'
    Character used to quote fields containing the delimiter
    or newlines (RFC 4180).

source : Path
    Path to csv file.

Contains.title: Contains
Contains.description: Check if sequence value contains element.

Defined.title: Defined
Defined.description: Check if state has specified key.

Eq.title: Eq
Eq.description: Check if values are equal using '==' operator.

FileNode.title: FileNode
FileNode.description:
Representation of a file or directory in a file tree.

Used as a recursive structure to describe the contents of a
generator directory for API responses.

Attributes
----------
name : str
    The base name of the file or directory.

is_dir : bool
    Whether this node represents a directory or a file.

children : list[FileNode] | None
    Nested file nodes if this node is a directory. `None` if this
    node is a file.

FormatErrorInfo.title: FormatErrorInfo
FormatErrorInfo.description:
Info about formatting errors.

Attributes
----------
message : str
    Error message.

original_event : str | None
    Original event.

FormatEventsBody.title: FormatEventsBody
FormatEventsBody.description:
Request body model containing info about formatter config
and events to format.

Attributes
----------
formatter_config : FormatterConfigT
    Formatter config.

events : list[str]
    Events to format.

FormattingResult.title: FormattingResult
FormattingResult.description:
Response model containing info about events formatting.

Attributes
----------
events : list[str]
    List of formatted events, number of events can be the same as
    original or not due to formatting errors of specific events
    from the entire provided list or reduction behavior of specific
    formatters which take multiple events and produce for example
    one aggregated event.

formatted_count : int
    Number of successfully formatted events, this field is helpful
    for tracking number of successfully formatted events with taking
    into account possible events aggregation.

errors : list[FormatErrorInfo]
    List with formatting errors of specific events or entire
    sequence of events (for specific aggregating formatters).

Ge.title: Ge
Ge.description:
Check if value is greater or equal to other value using '>='
operator.

GenerationParameters.title: GenerationParameters
GenerationParameters.description:
Generation parameters that are common for all generators and can
be overridden from generator parameters level.

Attributes
----------
timezone : str, default='UTC'
    Time zone for generating timestamps.

batch : BatchParameters, default=BatchParameters(...)
    Batch parameters.

queue : QueueParameters, default=QueueParameters(...)
    Queue parameters.

keep_order : bool, default=False
    Whether to keep chronological order of events using their
    timestamps by disabling output plugins concurrency.

max_concurrency : int, default=100
    Maximum number of write operations performed by output plugins
    concurrently.

write_timeout : int, default=10
    Timeout (in seconds) before canceling single write task.

size_in_bytes.title: Size In Bytes
size_in_bytes.description: Size of directory content in bytes

last_modified.title: Last Modified
last_modified.description: Last directory content modification time as unix timestamp

generator_ids.title: Generator Ids
generator_ids.description: IDs of generators that use configuration from this directory

GeneratorDirExtendedInfo.title: GeneratorDirExtendedInfo
GeneratorDirExtendedInfo.description: Response model that contains extended info about generator directory.

GeneratorParameters.title: GeneratorParameters
GeneratorParameters.description:
Parameters for single generator.

Attributes
----------
id : str
    Generator unique identified.

path : Path
    Path to configuration.

live_mode : bool, default=True
    Whether to use live mode and generate events at moments defined
    by timestamp values or sample mode to generate all events at a
    time.

skip_past : bool, default=True
    Whether to skip past timestamps when starting generation in
    live mode.

params: dict[str, Any], default={}
    Parameters that can be used in generator configuration file.

total_generated.title: Total Generated
total_generated.description: Total number of timestamps generated across all input plugins.

total_written.title: Total Written
total_written.description: Total number of written event across all output plugins.

uptime.title: Uptime
uptime.description: Number of seconds since generator start time.

input_eps.title: Input Eps
input_eps.description:
Average number of events per second for total number
of generated timestamps since generator start time.

output_eps.title: Output Eps
output_eps.description:
Average number of events per second for total number
of written events since generator start time.

is_ended_up.title: Is Ended Up
is_ended_up.description: Whether the generator has ended execution with or without errors

is_ended_up_successfully.title: Is Ended Up Successfully
is_ended_up_successfully.description: Whether the generator has ended execution successfully

GlobalsUsageResponse.description: Detected globals usage across all templates in a generator.

GlobalsWarningResponse.title: GlobalsWarningResponse
GlobalsWarningResponse.description: A warning about globals usage that cannot be fully detected.

Gt.description: Check if value is greater than other value using '>' operator.

HasTags.title: HasTags
HasTags.description: Check if event has specific tag.

uptime.title: Uptime
uptime.description: Number of seconds since host boot up.

PathParameters.title: PathParameters
PathParameters.description:
Path parameters.

Attributes
----------
logs : Path
    Absolute path to logs directory.

startup : Path
    Absolute path to file with list of generators to run at startup.

generators_dir : Path
    Absolute path to directory with generators configuration files.

keyring_cryptfile : Path
    Absolute path to keyring encrypted file with stored secrets

generator_config_filename : Path, default='generator.yml'
    Filename for generator configurations. This parameter is used
    by the API for detection directories with generator
    configurations. Directory with generator configuration named
    other than this parameter value will not be operable using API
    endpoints.

ProduceEventErrorInfo.title: ProduceEventErrorInfo
ProduceEventErrorInfo.description:
Information about errors related to events producing.

Attributes
----------
index : int
    Index of params object from params list used to produce events
    for which producing is failed.

message : str
    Error message.

context : dict[str, Any]
    Error context.

ProduceParams.title: ProduceParams
ProduceParams.description:
Params for `produce` method of `EventPlugin`.

Attributes
----------
timestamp : str
    Timestamp of event.

tags : tuple[str, ...]
    Tags from input plugin that generated timestamp.

ProducedEventsInfo.title: ProducedEventsInfo
ProducedEventsInfo.description:
Response model containing info about event produced by event
plugin.

Attributes
----------
events : list[str]
    Successfully produced events.

errors : list[ProduceEventErrorInfo]
    List of errors related to events producing.

exhausted : bool
    Whether the event plugin was exhausted during events producing.

QueueParameters.title: QueueParameters
QueueParameters.description:
Parameters of input plugins queue.

Attributes
----------
max_timestamp_batches : int, default=10
    Maximum number of batches in timestamps queue.

max_event_batches : int, default=10
    Maximum number of batches in events queue.

SSLParameters.title: SSLParameters
SSLParameters.description:
SSL parameters.

Attributes
----------
enabled : bool, default=False
    Whether to enable SSL.

verify_mode : Literal['none', 'optional', 'required'], default=None
    Verification mode of SSL connections. The default value `None`
    is same as Literal['none'], but `None` can also be used as
    meaningful value of "not provided" when SSL is disabled.

ca_cert: Path | None, default=None
    Absolute path to CA certificate.

cert: Path | None, default=None
    Absolute path to server certificate.

cert_key: Path | None, default=None
    Absolute path to server certificate key.

generator_ids.title: Generator Ids
generator_ids.description: IDs of generators in this scenario

ScenarioResponse.title: ScenarioResponse
ScenarioResponse.description: A scenario with its generator IDs.

ServerParameters.title: ServerParameters
ServerParameters.description:
Server parameters.

Attributes
----------
ui_enabled : bool, default = True
    Whether to enable web UI.

api_enabled : bool, default = True
    Whether to enable REST API.

host : str, default='0.0.0.0'
    Bind address for server process.

port : int, default=9474
    Bind port for server process,

ssl : SSLParameters, default=SSLParameters(...)
    SSL parameters.

auth : AuthParameters
    Auth parameters.

Settings-Input.title: Settings

Main settings of application.

----------
server: ServerParameters
    Server parameters.

generation: GenerationParameters
    Generation parameters.

log : LogParameters

path : PathParameters
    Path parameters.

Settings-Output.title: Settings

Main settings of application.

Attributes
----------
server: ServerParameters
    Server parameters.

generation: GenerationParameters
    Generation parameters.

log : LogParameters
    Log parameters.

path : PathParameters
    Path parameters.

Startup parameters for single generator.

autostart : bool, default=True
    Whether to automatically start the generator.
scenarios : list[str], default=[]
    Scenario names this generator belongs to.

Template configuration for `fsm` picking mode.

Attributes

    Transition configurations.

initial : bool, default=False
    Whether to define state as initial.

Template configuration for general picking modes.

vars : dict[str, Any]

TemplateTransition-Output.description:
Transition configuration for `fsm` picking mode.

VersatileDatetimeParametersBody.title: VersatileDatetimeParametersBody
VersatileDatetimeParametersBody.description:
Request body containing info for normalizing versatile datetime.

Attributes
----------
value : VersatileDatetime
    Value to normalize.

timezone : str
    Timezone that is used for returned datetime object.

relative_base : datetime | None
    Base time to use when value represents relative time, default
    is current time.

none_point : NonePoint, default='now'
    What time to use when `value` parameter is `None`: 'now' -
    current time; `min` - minimal value of datetime; `max` -
    maximal value of datetime.

eventum__plugins__event__plugins__replay.title: ReplayEventPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__event__plugins__replay.description:
Configuration for `replay` event plugin.

Attributes
----------
path : Path
    Path to log file.

timestamp_pattern : str | None, default=None
    Regular expression pattern to identify the timestamp
    substitution position within the original message. The
    substitution is performed over the named group `timestamp`.
    If value is not set or pattern does not match, then
    substitution is not performed. For more information about
    python regex syntax see:
    https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html#regular-expression-syntax

timestamp_format : str | None, default=None
    Format string that defines how the actual timestamp should be
    substituted in the log message. The format follows C89 standard.
    For more information see:
    https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#strftime-and-strptime-format-codes
    If value is not set, then default (ISO 8601) format is used.

repeat : bool, default=False
    Whether to repeat replaying after the end of file is reached.

chunk_size : int, default = 1_048_576
    Number of bytes to read from the file at a time. This parameter
    controls how often to access file and how many data will be
    stored in in memory. If 0 is provided then the entire file is
    read at once.

encoding : Encoding, default='utf_8'
    Encoding of the log file.

eventum__plugins__event__plugins__script.title: ScriptEventPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__event__plugins__script.description:
Configuration for `script` event plugin.

Attributes
----------
path : Path
    Path to script.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__cron__.title: CronInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__cron__.description:
Configuration for `cron` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
start : VersatileDatetime, default = None
    Start of the generating date range, if not set, current time
    is used.

end : VersatileDatetime, default = None
    End of the generating date range, if not set, then never end
    generation.

expression : str
    Cron expression (supports specifying seconds, years, random
    values and keywords, see more here:
    https://pypi.org/project/croniter/#about-second-repeats)

count : int
    Number of timestamps to generate for every interval.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__http__.title: HttpInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__http__.description:
Configuration for `http` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
host : str, default='0.0.0.0'
    Bind address.

port : int
    Bind port.

max_pending_requests : int, default=100
    Maximum number of incoming requests to store in queue before
    they are processed. If a request is received and the queue is
    full a 429 response will be returned immediately.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__linspa.title: LinspaceInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__linspa.description:
Configuration for `linspace` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
start : VersatileDatetimeStrict
    Start of the generating date range.

end : VersatileDatetimeStrict
    End of the generating date range.

count : int
    Number of events within date range.

endpoint : bool
    Whether to include end point of date range.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__static.title: StaticInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__static.description:
Configuration for `static` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
count : int
    Number of events to generate.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__time_p.title: TimePatternsInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__time_p.description:
Configuration for `time_patterns` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
patterns : list[Path]
    File paths to time pattern configurations.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__timer_.title: TimerInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__timer_.description:
Configuration for `timer` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
start : VersatileDatetime
    Start time of timer countdown, if not set current time is used.

seconds : float
    Number of seconds to wait before generating timestamp.

count : int
    Number of timestamps to generate.

repeat : int | None
    Number of cycles to repeat, if value is not set, then repeat
    infinitely.

eventum__plugins__input__plugins__timest.title: TimestampsInputPluginConfig
eventum__plugins__input__plugins__timest.description:
Configuration for `timestamps` input plugin.

Attributes
----------
source : list[datetime] | Path
    List of timestamps or path to file with new line separated
    timestamps in ISO8601 format.

Notes
-----
It is expected that timestamps are already sorted in ascending
order.

eventum__plugins__output__fields__JsonFo.title: JsonFormatterConfig
eventum__plugins__output__fields__JsonFo.description:
Config for json-like formats.

Parameters
----------
format : Literal[Format.JSON, Format.JSON_BATCH]
    Target format.

indent : int, default=0
    Indentation size.

eventum__plugins__output__fields__Simple.title: SimpleFormatterConfig
eventum__plugins__output__fields__Simple.description:
Config for formats without additional parameters.

format : Literal[Format.PLAIN, Format.EVENTUM_HTTP_INPUT]
    Target format.

eventum__plugins__output__fields__Templa.title: TemplateFormatterConfig
eventum__plugins__output__fields__Templa.description:
Config for template-like formats.

Parameters
----------
format : Literal[Format.TEMPLATE, Format.TEMPLATE_BATCH]
    Target format.

template : str | None, default=None
    Template content.

template_path : Path | None, default=None
    Template path.

Notes
-----
Template and template path are mutually exclusive parameters.

To access original event (for `template` mode) or events sequence
(for `template-batch` mode) use `event` or `events` variable in
template correspondingly.
